commit.message:
feat(cli): add XDG Base Directory config support (#12)

* feat(cli): add XDG Base Directory config support

Load persistent configuration from ~/.config/yaai/config.json5,
following the XDG Base Directory Specification to avoid config
file clutter in home directories.

All options (model, traces_dir, json_logs) can be set in the config
file and overridden at runtime via CLI args, following the resolution
order: CLI args > ~/.config/yaai/config.json5 > hardcoded defaults.

JSON5 is used over plain JSON to support comments and trailing commas,
which are essential for a human-authored config file.

- Add config crate (json5 feature) and dirs crate to workspace deps
- Add apps/cli/src/config.rs with YaaiConfig and load() fn
- Make --model and --traces-dir optional on PromptArgs
- Add PromptArgs::resolve() to apply layered config merging
- Wire config load and json_logs fallback into main
- Add tests for config parsing, resolve priority, and JSON5 syntax

* fix(cli): tilde expansion, dynamic config path in help text, and load() test

- Add expand_tilde() to PromptArgs::resolve() so traces_dir values like
  ~/... from config file are expanded to absolute paths
- Update --model and --traces-dir help strings and the missing-model error
  to reference $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/yaai/config.json instead of the
  hardcoded ~/.config/... path which is platform-specific
- Replace missing_file_returns_default test (which never called load())
  with load_returns_default_when_config_file_absent, which sets HOME and
  XDG_CONFIG_HOME to a temp dir and calls load() directly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cli): show real config path in help and error messages

Add config_path() and config_path_display() to config.rs. The display
helper returns the path with the home prefix replaced by ~ so it's
readable regardless of OS (e.g. ~/Library/Application Support/yaai/config.json
on macOS, ~/.config/yaai/config.json on Linux). Use mut_arg in main()
to inject the real path into --model, --traces-dir, and --json-logs help
text. Use config_path_display() in the missing-model error message.

---------

* fix(notebook): Configure memory mode and inject memory_id to prevent runtime failures

configure() defaults to memory_mode="NO_MEMORY", so the auto-created
execution role has no memory IAM permissions — causing ListMemoryEvents
failures at runtime. Additionally, the toolkit doesn't know about the
manually-created memory resource, so it provisions a duplicate on launch.

Fix: Set memory_mode="STM_ONLY" in configure() and inject the existing
memory_id into .bedrock_agentcore.yaml before launch(). Both issues only
exist because the tutorial manually creates resources that the toolkit
normally manages end-to-end.

commit.message:
Fix wrong error code for expired txs and add regression tests

The first validate_time_bounds call in queue admission mapped all errors
to TxTooEarly, but validate_time_bounds can also return TooLate (when
max_time < lcl_close_time). Match on the actual ValidationError variant
to return the correct TxResultCode.

Also strengthen the existing AUDIT-093 test to assert the specific
TxTooLate error code, and add a new test for already-expired transactions
that would have caught the wrong-error-code bug.

commit.message:
Fix [AUDIT-093]: Use close-time offsets for queue admission and nomination trimming

Queue admission now rejects transactions whose max_time will expire before
the estimated next ledger close, using upper_bound_offset = expected_close * 2
+ drift (parity: stellar-core getUpperBoundCloseTimeOffset).

Nomination tx-set building now computes close_time BEFORE building the tx set
and passes the offset to trimming (parity: stellar-core triggerNextLedger
computes nextCloseTime first, then passes offset to makeTxSetFromTransactions).

Chore: upgrade ts (#122)

* chore: upgrade TypeScript to v6

- Bump typescript dependency from ^5.7.3 to ^6.0.0
- Remove deprecated downlevelIteration option (only applied to ES5 target, which is deprecated in TS6)
- Replace deprecated moduleResolution 'node' (node10) with 'bundler' in all CJS tsconfigs
- Add skipLibCheck to tsconfig.base.json to resolve @types/node URLPattern conflict with TS6 DOM lib

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: avoid skipLibCheck by removing dom lib and adding @types/node to types

The previous commit added skipLibCheck to work around a type conflict
between TypeScript 6's DOM lib URLPattern declaration and @types/node@24's
conflicting declaration in its url module's global augmentation.

The proper fix: this is a Node.js library that doesn't need the browser
DOM lib. Removing 'dom' and 'esnext.disposable' from lib eliminates the
conflict at its source since URLPattern is then declared only once
(by @types/node). Adding 'node' to the types array ensures @types/node's
web platform globals (AbortSignal, EventTarget, WebSocket, setTimeout, etc.)
are available, which are needed by vitest and tinybench during type checking.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add intake consent packet and supplemental ref data (#95)

* Add intake consent packet and supplemental ref data

Introduce canonical intake consent packet support and land an asset-backed supplemental reference-data pipeline with provenance. Key changes: prefer IntakeConsentPacket over legacy consents JSON (DTOs, ResolveConsentJson, TryParseConsentPacket, endpoint normalization); expand IntakeStructuredData to include comorbidities, assistive devices, living situations and house-layout options and normalize them; add an embedded IntakeSupplementalReferenceData.json and loader, asset models, and ReferenceDataProvenance; wire provenance into IntakeReferenceDataCatalogService and expose supplemental option lookups; add workspace placeholders (ExerciseRows, GeneralInterventions) and logic to resolve canonical labels; update project to embed the supplemental JSON and add tests covering normalization and provenance. Files touched include DTOs, endpoints, structured-data models/normalization, reference-data services, infrastructure loaders/assets, workspace contracts/service, and tests.

* Update src/PTDoc.Infrastructure/ReferenceData/EmbeddedJsonResourceLoader.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve embedded JSON resource loading

Resolve the application assembly via IIntakeReferenceDataCatalogService instead of typeof(T) and introduce TryOpenResourceStream. The new helper first attempts a direct GetManifestResourceStream, then falls back to matching resource names by filename suffix (using the last two dotted segments) to handle differently prefixed embedded resource names. Also added the necessary using for PTDoc.Application.ReferenceData.
